mergeRationale: Save as new creates a new object, so exposing it to users with only change permission would allow unauthorized object creation.

requiredFindings.summary: The patch allows users with only change permission to access Save as new.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Save as new creates a new object. It must require add permission, and usually the existing change context as well, otherwise admin users can exceed their authorization.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Keep has_add_permission in the condition and ensure the POST path enforces the same authorization boundary.

requiredFindings.summary: The added test expects the unsafe button to be visible for a change-only user.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: The correct regression test should assert that users without add permission cannot see or perform Save as new, and that add+change users still can.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add negative UI and POST tests for change-only users, plus a positive test for users with both permissions.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because Save as new appears on the change form.
Showing the button is harmless because a later save path will handle permissions.
